Notice Of Motion And Motion To Deem The Truth Of Matters Specified In Defendant?S Request For Admissions, Set One, Admitted And Conclusively Established
Off Calendar Jan-12-2024 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

HEARING RE: DEFENDANT ZU OUYANG's Motion To Deem The Truth Of Matters Specified In Defendant's Request For Admissions, Set One, Admitted And Conclusively Established.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

The Motion to Deem the Truth of Matters Specified to Defendant Zu Pei Ouyang's Request for Admissions, Set One is GRANTED. The Court finds that no responses have been served by the Plaintiffs, leaving the Court with no discretion but to grant this motion and deem the truth of the matters specified admitted. (Demyer v. Costa Mesa Mobile Home Estates (1995) 36 Cal. App. 4th 393, 395-396 (disapproved on other grounds of by Wilcox v. Birtwhistle (1999) 21 Cal. 4th 973, 983).) It is so ORDERED.

Court to sign propsoed order submitted by the prevailing party.

Judge Pro Tem: Peter Van Zandt; Clerk: S. Kane; Not Reported. (302/JPT)
